title: Claude Code Plugin
description: Schema for [Claude Code plugin](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins) manifest (`.claude-plugin/plugin.json`). Plugins extend Claude Code with reusable skills, agents, hooks, MCP servers, and LSP servers that can be shared across projects and teams via [marketplaces](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugin-marketplaces). The manifest defines plugin metadata and optionally declares custom component paths. Components in default locations (`commands/`, `agents/`, `skills/`, `hooks/hooks.json`, `.mcp.json`, `.lsp.json`) are auto-discovered even without a manifest. The manifest is optional — if omitted, the directory name becomes the plugin name. See [plugin manifest schema](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference#plugin-manifest-schema) for the complete reference.

name.description: Unique identifier for the plugin (kebab-case, no spaces). This name **namespaces all plugin components** — skills appear as `/plugin-name:skill-name`, agents as `plugin-name:agent-name`, etc. Users see this in install commands (e.g. `/plugin install my-plugin@marketplace`). If the manifest is omitted entirely, the directory name is used instead.

version.description: Semantic version (`MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`). Claude Code uses this to detect updates — if you change code without bumping the version, existing users **won't see updates** due to [caching](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference#plugin-caching-and-file-resolution). If also set in a marketplace entry, the `plugin.json` version takes priority. Use pre-release versions like `2.0.0-beta.1` for testing. See [version management](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference#version-management).

description.description: Brief explanation of the plugin's purpose. Shown in the plugin manager when browsing or installing plugins.
examples: A greeting plugin to learn the basics

homepage.description: URL to the plugin's documentation or landing page.

repository.description: URL to the plugin's source code repository.

keywords.description: Discovery tags for the plugin. Used when searching or browsing plugins in marketplaces.

commands.description: Additional command file or directory paths, relative to the plugin root starting with `./`. These **supplement** the default `commands/` directory — they don't replace it. Paths should point to `.md` files or directories containing `.md` files. See [path behavior rules](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference#path-behavior-rules).

agents.description: Additional agent file or directory paths, relative to the plugin root starting with `./`. These **supplement** the default `agents/` directory.

skills.description: Additional [skill](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills) directory paths, relative to the plugin root starting with `./`. These **supplement** the default `skills/` directory. Paths should point to directories containing `SKILL.md` files.

hooks.description: [Hook](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks) configuration — either paths to JSON config files or inline hook definitions. Custom paths **supplement** `hooks/hooks.json` (the default location). Inline definitions map event names to arrays of matcher groups.

oneOf.description: Path to a hooks JSON config file, relative to the plugin root.
Array of paths to hooks JSON config files.

mcpServers.description: [MCP server](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp) configuration — either paths to JSON config files or inline server definitions. Custom paths **supplement** `.mcp.json` (the default location). Inline definitions map server names to their transport configuration. MCP servers start automatically when the plugin is enabled. Use `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` for plugin-relative paths.

oneOf.description: Path to an MCP config JSON file, relative to the plugin root.
Array of paths to MCP config JSON files.
Inline MCP server definitions. Each key is a server name.
Reference to an already-configured MCP server by name.

outputStyles.description: Additional output style files or directories, relative to the plugin root starting with `./`.

lspServers.description: [LSP server](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference#lsp-servers) configuration — either paths to JSON config files or inline server definitions. Custom paths **supplement** `.lsp.json` (the default location). LSP integration provides real-time code intelligence: diagnostics, go to definition, find references, and hover information. Users must install the language server binary separately.

oneOf.description: Path to an LSP config JSON file, relative to the plugin root.
Array of paths to LSP config JSON files.
Inline LSP server definitions. Each key is a language name.
A single relative path starting with `./`.
Array of relative paths, each starting with `./`.

McpServerConfig.description: [MCP server](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp) configuration for a stdio-based server process. Use `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` for plugin-relative paths.

command.description: Executable to run for the MCP server. Use `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` for plugin-relative paths.

args.description: Arguments to pass to the command.

env.description: Environment variables for the server process. Values support `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}`.

cwd.description: Working directory for the server process.

LspServerConfig.description: [LSP server](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference#lsp-servers) configuration for a language. The language server binary must be installed separately on the user's machine. See [available LSP plugins](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference#lsp-servers) for pre-built options.

command.description: The LSP binary to execute (must be in `PATH`).

args.description: Command-line arguments for the LSP server.

env.description: Environment variables to set when starting the server.

initializationOptions.description: Options passed to the server during [initialization](https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/specifications/lsp/3.17/specification/#initialize).

workspaceFolder.description: Workspace folder path for the server.

startupTimeout.description: Max time to wait for server startup in milliseconds.

shutdownTimeout.description: Max time to wait for graceful shutdown in milliseconds.

restartOnCrash.description: Whether to automatically restart the server if it crashes.

maxRestarts.description: Maximum number of restart attempts before giving up.

Hooks.description: Map of [hook event](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#hook-events) names to arrays of matcher groups.

SessionStart.description: Fires when a session starts. Matcher filters by source: `startup`, `resume`, `clear`, `compact`.

UserPromptSubmit.description: Fires when the user submits a prompt. **No matcher support**.

PreToolUse.description: Fires before a tool is executed. Matcher filters by tool name (e.g. `Bash`, `Edit|Write`, `mcp__.*`).

PermissionRequest.description: Fires when a tool requests user permission. Matcher filters by tool name.

PostToolUse.description: Fires after a tool executes successfully. Matcher filters by tool name.

PostToolUseFailure.description: Fires after a tool execution fails. Matcher filters by tool name.

Notification.description: Fires on notifications. Matcher filters by type: `permission_prompt`, `idle_prompt`, `auth_success`, `elicitation_dialog`.

SubagentStart.description: Fires when a subagent is spawned. Matcher filters by agent type.

SubagentStop.description: Fires when a subagent stops. Matcher filters by agent type.

Stop.description: Fires when Claude's turn ends. **No matcher support**.

TeammateIdle.description: Fires when a teammate goes idle. **No matcher support**.

TaskCompleted.description: Fires when a task is marked completed. **No matcher support**.

ConfigChange.description: Fires when a configuration file changes during a session. Matcher filters by source: `user_settings`, `project_settings`, `local_settings`, `policy_settings`, `skills`.

PreCompact.description: Fires before context compaction. Matcher filters by trigger: `manual`, `auto`.

SessionEnd.description: Fires when a session ends. Matcher filters by exit reason.

MatcherGroup.description: A matcher group pairs an optional regex filter with an array of hook handlers. If `matcher` is omitted, hooks fire on every occurrence. See [matcher patterns](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#matcher-patterns).

matcher.description: Regex pattern to filter when hooks fire. Use `|` for OR, `.*` for wildcards. MCP tools: `mcp__<server>__<tool>`.

CommandHook.description: Runs a shell command or script. Receives [hook context as JSON on stdin](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#common-input-fields). **Exit 0**: success. **Exit 2**: blocking error. **Other**: non-blocking error.

command.description: Shell command or script path. Use `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` for plugin-relative paths.

statusMessage.description: Custom spinner message while the hook runs.

once.description: Run once per session then remove.

PromptHook.description: Evaluates a single-turn LLM [prompt-based hook](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#prompt-based-hooks). Must respond `{ok: true}` or `{ok: false, reason: "..."}`. Cannot use tools.

prompt.description: Evaluation prompt. Use `$ARGUMENTS` for hook input context.

model.description: Model for evaluation. Defaults to a fast model.

statusMessage.description: Custom spinner message while the hook runs.

once.description: Run once per session then remove.

AgentHook.description: Runs a multi-turn LLM [agent-based hook](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#agent-based-hooks) with tool access. Must respond `{ok: true}` or `{ok: false, reason: "..."}`. Can explore the codebase to verify conditions.

prompt.description: Verification prompt. Use `$ARGUMENTS` for hook input context.

model.description: Model for the agent. Defaults to a fast model.

statusMessage.description: Custom spinner message while the hook runs.

once.description: Run once per session then remove.
